description: This figure comprises two line graphs depicting the mechanical properties of as-cast Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C alloy sheet castings (where x = 5–40 mass%) designated as alloys A through H. Subfigure (a) displays the ultimate tensile strength (UTS, solid diamond markers) and yield strength (YS, solid square markers) versus alloy designation, while subfigure (b) presents the elongation (EL, solid diamond markers) and Rockwell hardness (HRC, solid square markers). The UTS and YS both exhibit a distinct peak at alloy C (15 mass% Mn) with values of approximately 887 MPa and 824 MPa, respectively, then gradually decline to 646 MPa and 575 MPa at alloy H (40 mass% Mn). In contrast, elongation shows a monotonic increasing trend from 16.3% to 29.2% as Mn content increases. Hardness follows a similar pattern to the strength properties, ranging from HRC 31 to 44 with the maximum occurring at 15 mass% Mn. These trends reflect the underlying microstructural transition from a duplex austenite (γ) and D03-ordered ferrite (α) mixture to a single austenite phase, with the (D03 + κ″) phase region playing a critical role in the strengthening mechanism.
